Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

This is a good optimization, but I think it should be trivial to just have a single init/fini pair, so I'd prefer that if possible...?  Could add a comment that the write_osclock only applies in the write() case (or also not - it's probably obvious).

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

I'm a bit worried that when there's not enough lru slots left every one is calling osc_lru_reclaim(), making the contention on cli->cl_lru_list_lock worse.
But I think benchmark and testing will tell us.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 0692dadfba87b963f1209defff81be7e6196c38e by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-12428 tests: wait for nodemaps to be synchronized

commit.subject: LU-12520 osc: reserve lru pages for read in batch
commit.message:
LU-12520 osc: reserve lru pages for read in batch

The benefit of doing this is to reduce contention
against atomic counter cl_lru_left by changing it from
per-page access to per-IO access.

We have done this optimization for write, do it for read
too.

Change-Id: Ifd15d0a59eda265dce43876d953e32f27b07b6a0
Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/35440
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
